title: Erdos Frontier Atlas entry
description: One machine-readable frontier record per audited Erdos problem. board_class is RECOMPUTED from the verifier and frontier fields by the R1-R4 rule below; it is not copied from any upstream triage. READY := (R1) exact integer/rational witness check, a-priori bounded on a byte-capped witness, <= ~1 s per candidate; (R2) a concrete OPEN numeric frontier that a single submitted finite witness strictly improves; (R3) witness conditions discrete or open/robust (strict inequalities), so integer/rational witnesses are lossless; (R4) one board per finite frontier object (duplicate entries cross-reference the carrier). HEAVY := exact adjudication exists but fails R1 timing (long MIS/SAT run per candidate) or the movable claim is certificate-shaped (DRAT chain, exhaustion receipt, SDP dual) - optimistic-oracle tier. NONE := no exact poly-time verifier in any movable direction, or witness not finitely representable, or no open finite frontier (asymptotic-only / solved / duplicate object).

prize.description: Erdos prize headline and its status. NOTE: no Erdos prize in this atlas is claimable by finite computation; this field records history, not a payout.

finite_object.description: The concrete finite object (if any) whose record moves

current_record.description: Current frontier: values, holders, years, methods, with sources

frontier.description: Frontier summary for boardable entries (READY/HEAVY); null for NONE

beatable.description: Audit verdict on whether OUR farm can move the record. Independent of board_class: a WALL for us can still be a READY market-maker board for someone else's construction.

verifier.description: Exact-verifier spec: what is checked, arithmetic class, per-candidate cost

attack.description: Attack sketch from the deep audit

campaign_finding.description: Dated empirical routing evidence from an attempted campaign; never changes board readiness by itself.

lane.description: Primary solver lane (shared infrastructure; see atlas/lanes.md)

board_class.description: Recomputed by the R1-R4 rule in this schema's description

board_class_reason.description: Which rule conjuncts pass/fail and why, per entry

wall_reason.description: For board_class NONE: the do-not-enter reason (mirrors atlas/walls.md)

p42_slug.description: Slug of the packaged P42 board under p42-prizes/problems/, if one exists
